Title: Forecasting Data Scientist
Description:
Are you a Data Scientist with strong forecasting experience who’s comfortable taking models from concept through to production?

This is an opportunity to work on real-world forecasting use cases within a large Energy organisation, where your work will directly support pricing, demand planning, and operational decisions.

You’ll be part of a cross-functional product team, working closely with Product Managers, Data Engineers, and Architects to build and deploy scalable data science solutions that are actually used - not just explored.




Your Role as a Data Scientist (Forecasting):

You will focus on building and delivering forecasting models that solve concrete business problems. This role spans the full lifecycle - from understanding requirements through to deployment and ongoing optimisation.

You’ll work closely with both technical and business stakeholders to ensure models are accurate, reliable, and aligned with how decisions are made.




What You’ll Do:

Develop and implement forecasting models using classical/statistical approaches (time series, regression-based methods)
Translate business problems (pricing, demand, parts forecasting) into practical data science solutions
Collaborate closely with Product Managers to align model outputs with business needs
Work alongside Data Engineers to ensure robust data pipelines and production-ready deployment
Contribute to MLOps practices including deployment, monitoring, and model lifecycle management
Engage with stakeholders to understand context and refine requirements
Partner with architects to align solutions with system architecture and scalability goals
Continuously improve model performance based on real-world usage




What You’ll Bring:

Strong experience in forecasting and time series modelling (classical/statistical approaches)
Proficiency in Python for data science and model development
Hands-on experience with AWS SageMaker for model development and deployment
Experience with Amazon Bedrock or similar platforms
Understanding of MLOps practices (CI/CD, model versioning, monitoring, deployment pipelines)
Experience working with production data systems and collaborating with Data Engineers
Ability to translate business problems into structured, data-driven solutions




Why Join the Remobi community:

Fully remote, b2b/freelance contracts
Opportunity to work on large-scale, high-impact transformation programmes
Access to a network of experienced data, AI, and engineering professionals across Europe




👉 Apply today:

If you’re comfortable owning forecasting models end-to-end and getting them into production, this is a strong fit.

Title: Data Scientist H/F
Description:
MCA BELGIUM, filiale du groupe français MCA, est une société de conseil en ingénierie et hautes technologies. Avec plus de 15 ans d’expérience en Belgique, nous accompagnons nos clients dans la réalisation de projets industriels complexes dans divers secteurs d’activité (énergie, nucléaire, pharmaceutique, aéronautique, ferroviaire).

Nous rejoindre, c’est intégrer une entreprise dynamique qui place l’accompagnement de ses collaborateurs au cœur de ses priorités. Vous bénéficierez d’un suivi personnalisé et de nombreuses formations pour développer vos compétences.

Votre Rôle

Rattaché(e) au pôle Data & Engineering, vous intervenez sur l’exploitation avancée de données industrielles issues d’équipements d’énergies renouvelables (solaire & éolien), avec pour objectif d’optimiser la performance des installations et d’anticiper les défaillances :


Analyse de données provenant de machines industrielles (production, capteurs, historiques de fonctionnement)
Conception et développement de modèles statistiques et algorithmiques (machine learning, détection d’anomalies)
Identification des pertes de performance, anomalies et risques de panne
Participation à la mise en place de solutions de maintenance prédictive
Contribution à l’optimisation du potentiel des équipements
Restitution des résultats via rapports techniques et documentation associée
Collaboration avec les équipes multidisciplinaires (ingénierie, experts terrain, métiers)
Veille sur les évolutions technologiques et méthodologiques en data science appliquée à l’industrie


Votre Profil


Diplôme d’ingénieur ou formation équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation en data science, mathématiques appliquées ou domaine proche
Première expérience professionnelle en data (stage long, VIE ou premier poste accepté), avec exposition concrète à des projets data
Maîtrise de Python
Bonne compréhension des algorithmes, statistiques et approches machine learning
Esprit analytique, rigueur et sens du travail en équipe
Maîtrise du français & anglais professionnel


Vos Perspectives D’évolution

Nous souhaitons vous accompagner dans votre montée en compétences. Notre objectif est de vous donner toutes les clés pour réussir et accéder aux différentes opportunités d’évolution. Vous pourrez évoluer vers des fonctions techniques, de gestion de projet ou de management. Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.

Title: Data Scientist (Junior) - DVW000533
Description:
About The Job Data Scientist (Junior) - DVW000533

Data Scientist (Junior)

Flanders, Belgium

Start Date: May 2026

Overview

We are looking for a Junior Data Scientist to join a collaborative team working on data-driven solutions within a dynamic, infrastructure-focused environment. You will support the processing, analysis, and management of large datasets, contributing to the optimization of data flows and systems.

Key Responsibilities


Support and maintain data workflows using FME
Process, analyze, and manage large datasets from multiple sources
Work with SQL Server to store, query, and optimize data
Analyze data streams and contribute to data-driven insights
Collaborate with team members to develop and implement data solutions
Assist in improving database performance and query efficiency


Requirements


Experience with SQL Server and database optimization (minimum 1 year)
Experience analyzing data and data streams
Hands-on experience with Dagster
Basic knowledge of GIS (Geographic Information Systems)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to work in a team-oriented, project-based environment
Dutch language proficiency at C2 level (mandatory)


Nice to Have


Experience with ETL processes and reporting
Knowledge of scripting languages (Python, Shell, Perl)
Familiarity with Agile methodologies
Understanding of business processes or public sector environments
Experience working in small teams


Additional Details


Full-time role (100%)
Hybrid working model (remote work possible)
Contract until June 2027


If you are a motivated junior professional with a passion for data and analytics, this role offers an excellent opportunity to grow your skills in a real-world, impactful environment.

Title: Lead Scientist
Description:
Ready to architect the future of human-computer voice interaction?




Join an established conversational AI company as they transition from traditional cascaded speech systems to cutting-edge E2E speech-to-speech technology. You'll lead this transformation, building multimodal systems that will redefine how millions interact with AI.




The opportunity

You'll be leading the development of speech technology that directly impacts real users at massive scale. The company processes millions of daily interactions across major enterprise clients, meaning your research will shape real-world conversational experiences.




You'll spearhead the development of full-duplex speech systems, creating truly natural AI conversations that go far beyond current capabilities.




Your impact

Design and build next-generation multimodal speech LLM architecture from the ground up
Drive breakthroughs in speech-to-speech modeling and full-duplex conversation systems
Tackle turn-taking, interruption handling, and simultaneous speech processing
Bridge cutting-edge research with enterprise-grade production systems
Lead a growing team focused on SOTA speech-to-speech breakthroughs and own the development end-to-end




What you'll bring

Deep understanding of SOTA speech models and neural audio processing
Experience building speech language models/multimodal systems
Strong background in speech AI research and modern speech architectures




This is all underpinned by access to a large corpus of real enterprise conversational data and serious GPU infrastructure.




The company has built everything in-house, giving you complete technical control and the freedom to explore any approach that delivers value.




With their established market position and proven track record, you'll have the resources and real-world testing ground to make transformative impact with your research.




Location

Remote (Must be within EU timezone).

This is all underpinned by access to a large corpus of real enterprise conversational data and serious GPU infrastructure.


The company has built everything in-house, giving you complete technical control and the freedom to explore any approach that delivers value.


With their established market position and proven track record, you'll have the resources and real-world testing ground to make transformative impact with your research.


Location

Remote (Must be within EU timezone).

Title: AI & Optimization Engineer
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI & Optimization Engineer in Belgium.

This is an exciting opportunity to work at the intersection of artificial intelligence, optimization, and large-scale infrastructure operations within a globally distributed remote environment. In this role, you will help transform complex operational data into intelligent systems that drive automation, predictive insights, and efficiency improvements across high-performance environments. You will collaborate closely with engineering, data, and operational teams to design scalable AI solutions and optimize real-world systems using advanced machine learning and data modeling techniques. The position offers the chance to work on cutting-edge technologies involving time-series data, IoT-style telemetry, and production-grade AI deployment. Ideal for professionals passionate about innovation and applied AI, this role combines strategic thinking with hands-on technical execution in a fast-moving and highly collaborative setting.

Accountabilities


Define and execute AI and optimization strategies focused on improving operational efficiency and measurable business performance.
Design and maintain scalable data models, schemas, and telemetry structures to support AI and machine learning initiatives.
Develop, deploy, and optimize machine learning models for anomaly detection, predictive maintenance, automation, performance optimization, and operational forecasting.
Collaborate with engineering teams to integrate AI capabilities into existing systems, workflows, APIs, and production environments.
Continuously monitor and improve model performance, reliability, explainability, and scalability in live operational settings.
Work closely with cross-functional stakeholders to translate operational challenges into data-driven solutions and actionable insights.
Document architectures, modelling assumptions, and AI workflows clearly while promoting a data-driven and innovation-focused culture.
Stay informed on emerging trends in AI, optimization, industrial analytics, and automation technologies.


Requirements


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Engineering, Statistics, or a related technical field.
At least 3 years of experience in AI/ML engineering, applied optimization, data science, or related analytical roles.
Strong expertise in Python and modern machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Scikit-Learn.
Proficiency in JavaScript or TypeScript within Node.js environments.
Experience designing and managing large-scale, time-series-focused data architectures and pipelines.
Solid understanding of optimization techniques, predictive modelling, anomaly detection, and industrial or telemetry-based AI systems.
Familiarity with Docker and containerized deployment environments.
Experience deploying, monitoring, and maintaining production AI systems, including handling model drift and changing operational conditions.
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and the ability to work independently in globally distributed remote teams.
Excellent English communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and non-technical stakeholders.


Benefits


100% remote work environment with global collaboration opportunities.
Opportunity to work on innovative AI and optimization projects with real-world operational impact.
Exposure to cutting-edge technologies in machine learning, automation, infrastructure, and distributed systems.
Fast-paced and highly collaborative international team culture.
Career growth opportunities within a rapidly evolving and innovation-driven industry.
Flexible and autonomous work environment that encourages creativity and ownership.
Work alongside highly skilled professionals in engineering, AI, and infrastructure operations.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
🤖 The Job

At Dataroots, we couple our excellent consulting services with our technical expertise to build advanced machine learning models to improve business processes across a variety of industries, with a strong focus on DataOps and MLOps. As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll be an integral part of our in-house team of AI specialists, designing and implementing models that deliver robust, data-driven solutions for our clients.




Passionate about data, you enjoy diving deep to uncover actionable insights and develop machine learning models that enhance client operations. You’ll experiment with different approaches, while collaborating with Data Engineers, Cloud Architects, and Data Strategists to drive the delivery of tailored, high-quality, end-to-end solutions.




You’ll also have the opportunity to mentor colleagues, share knowledge, and stay on top of industry developments through a range of initiatives we support internally.




🎉 What you'll find at Dataroots

Since 2016, Dataroots has been committing its excellent consulting services to help clients excel in their data-driven operations. Our approach focuses on team well-being, fostering diversity, and promoting knowledge sharing. By focusing on these values, we've achieved healthy and sustainable growth over the years. Through our consulting services, we actively encourage professional growth by exposing our employees to different industries, tools, technologies,... broadening their scope in the process.

As part of the Talan Group, we are able to take this approach to the next level, adding the element of international mobility to our employees’ career journey.

Join a team where your work will have a direct impact on both our clients and our journey. Learn more about our DNA at [https://dataroots.io/our-dna].




💼 What we’re looking for

You bring the following qualities & experiences to the role:

A deep passion for Machine Learning and a Master’s or PhD in a quantitative discipline.
Strong foundation in software design principles.
A minimum of 2 years of experience in machine learning and big data.
Expertise in machine learning and deep learning, with both a theoretical and practical grasp.
Proficiency in Python & SQL is a must; knowledge of R is an advantage.
Experience with deploying machine learning models and familiarity with MLOps practices.
At least a first experience with AWS, Azure or GCP and a solid understanding of cloud engineering and its use in Machine Learning.
Experience with ML versioning tooling.
Enthusiasm for working with large datasets and a pragmatic approach to problem-solving.
Strong communication skills and the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for clients.
Fluency in English and Dutch.




Don't worry if you don’t meet every requirement from the start. Most of all we are looking for colleagues with the same passion for personal and team development as the rest of us.




💰 The Offer

An attractive salary with extralegal benefits, including:

+ Mobility budget or a company car with fuel/charging card

+ Hospitalisation and group insurance

+ High-end laptop

+ Smartphone with subscription

+ Substantial amount of holidays

+ Meal vouchers

+ ...

Diverse and welcoming work environment, where you’ll collaborate & unwind with colleagues from different cultures and disciplines. The organization of both fun & professional events and initiatives is actively encouraged and supported.
A training budget for individual and team learning opportunities.
Tons of team-building events and sports initiatives to stay connected and unwind.




📍 Where you’ll work

Our main offices are in Leuven and Ghent, with co-working spaces in Charleroi and Antwerp. These spaces are perfect for both focusing and unwinding with colleagues, as well as brainstorming sessions and team events. While much of your work can be performed remotely, you will also work on-site with clients.




🎯 Ready to join Dataroots?

Excited to shape the future of AI as a Machine Learning Engineer with us? We’re looking forward to hearing from you! Apply now and start a new chapter with Dataroots! 🎉

Title: Quantum Research Scientist with Python - Freelance AI Trainer
HTML:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:

  • Design original computational physics problems that simulate real physics research workflows;
  • Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Numpy, SciPy, Sympy);
  • Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks);
  • Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in mechanics, electromagnetism, thermodynamics, and quantum mechanics;
  • Base problems on real research challenges or practical applications from physics practice;
  • Verify solutions using Python with standard physics simulation libraries;
  • Document problem statements clearly and provide verified correct answers

What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for quantum researchers with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:

  • Degree in Physics (Theoretical, Experimental, or Computational) or related fields;
  • Python proficiency for numerical validation. MATLAB, R, C, SQL, Numpy, Pandas, SciPy, domain-specific libraries, Stata or knowledge of any programming language can be equivalent;
  • 2+ years of professional experience: applied, research, or teaching experience is applicable;
  • Experience with numerical simulation methods;
  • Ability to design problems that mirror real physics research workflows;
  • Creative thinking in problem design across diverse physics areas;
  • Familiarity with physics modeling and approximation techniques;
  • Strong written English (C1+)

How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $45 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer

I’m currently working with a fast-growing, well-funded tech company that’s building a cutting-edge platform to automate and optimize cloud-native and AI infrastructure at scale. They’ve recently hit unicorn status and are continuing to scale their engineering teams globally.




They’re now hiring a Senior ML / AI Engineer to join a high-impact R&D team focused on improving how large language models are deployed and used in production. 🌍 Remote (Europe, GMT 0–3)

Title: Freelance Data Scraping Engineer (Python)
Description:
Mindrift is looking for highly skilled Python Data Scraping Engineers to join the Tendem project and drive specialized data scraping workflows within our hybrid AI + human system.

In this role, as an AI Pilot - that's how we refer to this role at Mindrift - you'll collaborate with Tendem Agents that handle repetitive tasks, while you provide critical thinking, domain expertise, and quality control to deliver accurate and actionable results.

This part-time remote opportunity is ideal for technical professionals with hands-on experience in web scraping, data extraction and processing.

What We Do

The Mindrift platform connects specialists with AI projects from major tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the potential of Generative AI by tapping into real-world expertise from across the globe.

About The Role

This is a freelance role for a Tendem project. As a Python Data Scraping Engineer, you'll handle data scraping tasks requiring technical precision for web extraction and processing, utilizing various tools such as our provided Apify and OpenRouter alongside your own resourceful approaches.

Key Responsibilities


Own end-to-end data extraction workflows across complex websites, ensuring complete coverage, accuracy, and reliable delivery of structured datasets.
Leverage internal tools (Apify, OpenRouter) alongside custom workflows to accelerate data collection, validation, and task execution while meeting defined requirements.
Ensure reliable extraction from dynamic and interactive web sources, adapting approaches as needed to handle JavaScript-rendered content and changing site behavior.
Enforce data quality standards through validation checks, cross-source consistency controls, adherence to formatting specifications, and systematic verification prior to delivery.
Scale scraping operations for large datasets using efficient batching or parallelization, monitor failures, and maintain stability against minor site structure changes


Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $37 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

How To Get Started

Simply apply to this post, qualify, and get the chance to contribute to projects that match your technical skills, on your own schedule. From coding and automation to fine-tuning AI outputs, you'll play a key role in advancing AI capabilities and real-world applications.

Requirements


At least 3 year of relevant experience in data engineering, web scraping, automation, or software development (required).
Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Computer Science, or related technical fields is a plus
Strong experience in Python web scraping (BeautifulSoup, Selenium or similar), including dynamic content (JS, AJAX, infinite scroll) and APIs via proxies
Proven ability to extract data from complex structures (hierarchies, archived pages, inconsistent HTML)
Solid background in data cleaning, normalization, and validation, delivering structured datasets (CSV, JSON, Google Sheets)
Hands-on experience with LLMs and AI frameworks to enhance automation and problem-solving
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy
Self-directed work ethic with ability to troubleshoot independently
A link to GitHub is a plus
English proficiency: Upper-intermediate (B2) or above (required)


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Work fully remote on your own schedule with just a laptop and stable internet connection.
Gain hands-on experience in a unique hybrid environment where human expertise and AI agents collaborate seamlessly — a distinctive skill set in a rapidly growing field.
Participate in performance-based bonus programs that reward high-quality work and consistent delivery

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?

  • Work fully remote on your own schedule with just a laptop and stable internet connection.
  • Gain hands-on experience in a unique hybrid environment where human expertise and AI agents collaborate seamlessly — a distinctive skill set in a rapidly growing field.
  • Participate in performance-based bonus programs that reward high-quality work and consistent delivery

Title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Job Type: Permanent / Full-Time

Working Hours: 36–40 hours per week

Work Location: Brussels, Belgium

Working Model: Hybrid (50% on-site, 50% remote)

Experience Level: Experienced Professional




Role Overview

Machine Learning Engineers promote the adoption of best standards in industrial code development across the ML&AI community. They do so by developing ML pipelines that are production-ready by design or by integrating existing ML solutions into industrial pipelines.

They participate in the development, deployment and monitoring of AI services, which means they contribute to data quality checks, data flow design, the design of the models themselves and their overall integration into the production environment.

ML Engineers are meant to facilitate the communication between AI & Analytics teams and IT production with regards to the deployment of ML models, ensuring that models put in production are equipped with the appropriate data pipelines and monitoring.

Key Responsibilities

As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will:

Collaborate with Data Scientists to design and develop ML solutions with production constraints in mind.
Select appropriate infrastructure, serving models, and data ingestion approaches to meet business requirements such as real-time processing and high data volumes.
Automate and industrialize end-to-end ML pipelines, including:
Docker/VM image creation
Unit, regression, and integration testing
Continuous integration and deployment
Support Data Scientists in using existing industrial AI platforms and CI/CD tools for building and monitoring AI services.
Work closely with IT Production teams to configure and optimize target production environments.
Participate in the deployment, monitoring, and maintenance of AI/ML services.
Ensure proper data quality checks, data flow design, and model integration within production systems.




Mandatory Skills & Experience

Minimum 4 years of relevant experience as an ML Engineer or related role
Strong experience in Python (advanced level)
Experience with containerization and virtualization technologies
Hands-on experience with AI platforms and IDEs
CI/CD pipelines (especially GitLab CI)
Code, model, and data versioning
Package management tools and dependency management
Database experience with PostgreSQL
Solid understanding of Agile methodologies




Preferred / Nice‑to‑Have Skills

Experience with system integration across distributed systems, mainframe, and infrastructure components
Knowledge of model compression techniques
Experience with ELT / ETL tools
Exposure to Big Data technologies (e.g., Apache Spark)
Knowledge of data flow / stream processing
Familiarity with data visualization tools







Soft Skills

Strong verbal and written communication skills
Result‑oriented and delivery‑focused mindset
Attention to detail and strong analytical rigor
Creativity, innovation, and problem‑solving ability
Proactive approach to continuous learning and skill development
Awareness of efficiency and effectiveness
Ability to think outside existing processes and frameworks
Positive energy, ownership mindset, and strong collaboration skills
Open to change, feedback, and diverse viewpoints




Your home at Capgemini

You will join Capgemini Financial Services Benelux, working alongside passionate and skilled colleagues toward shared goals. Capgemini offers:

Structured onboarding and transparent growth paths
Flexibility to shape your own career journey
A culture of trust, collaboration, and innovation
Opportunities to work at the international forefront of technology
Strong commitment to sustainability, diversity, and inclusion




Working at Capgemini

Get the future you want. That's a promise we make to you. It starts on day one. With good onboarding and structured and transparent growth paths. But also: plenty of opportunities to deviate from these to pioneer and to choose your own path. You can count on our boundless trust and collegial support. Working for Capgemini also means working in the international heart of innovation. The premier league of technology. An employer with an eye for you and a sight on the future, in which sustainability and diversity and inclusion play the main roles.

Title: Clinical Scientist
Company: EORTC - European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer
Description:
The Clinical Scientist works under the hierarchical supervision of the Head of Medical Department.

Objective

The Clinical Scientist provides high‑level scientific, methodological, and operational support to EORTC clinical research activities. He/she contributes to the development, conduct, and dissemination of EORTC‑led studies and strategic research initiatives. The role supports disease‑specific and transversal research, including externally funded collaborative programmes, ensuring scientific quality, regulatory compliance, and alignment with EORTC strategy.

Main responsibilities / Major Activities

Main responsibilities


Contribute to the scientific conception, development, and implementation of EORTC clinical, methodological, and translational research projects.
Provide scientific leadership and coordination support to EORTC projects, in close collaboration with clinical research physicians, statisticians, project managers, and other HQ‑based experts.
Support externally funded collaborative projects, including EU‑ and IHI‑funded grants, by contributing to study design, methodological development, governance, reporting, and dissemination activities.
Ensure scientific consistency, quality, and documentation standards across assigned projects, in line with EORTC processes and regulatory requirements.


Major activities

New project development and strategic support


Provide strategic and operational scientific support to disease-oriented and transversal groups in the development of new research concepts.
Identify and synthesise relevant scientific information related to investigational therapies, biomarkers, imaging methodologies, response assessment criteria, and translational research opportunities.
Contribute to the development of study concepts and scientific rationales, in close collaboration with statisticians and clinical research physicians.
Support preliminary feasibility assessments and definition of project scope together with project management, operations, and regulatory teams.
Coordinate input from internal HQ teams and external experts to ensure high scientific quality of proposed projects.


Strategic review and endorsement of projects


Coordinate and support the scientific and strategic review of new study concepts within EORTC governance structures.
Support the identification of appropriate funding opportunities, including competitive grant calls and public–private partnerships.


Protocol, synopsis, and scientific documentation development


Provide scientific input into the development of synopses, protocols, amendments, and associated study documents.
Participate in the preparation of grant applications, including methodological sections, work packages, milestones, deliverables, and risk mitigation plans.


Study conduct and oversight


Contribute to scientific oversight activities during study conduct, in close collaboration with clinical research physicians and multidisciplinary operational teams (project management, regulatory affairs, pharmacovigilance, data management, monitoring).
Support methodological consistency and appropriate interpretation of clinical and translational study results.


Methodological research initiatives


Contribute to research activities related to the development, validation, and implementation clinical trial methodologies.
Participate in coordination of scientific activities across consortium partners, including working groups, advisory bodies, and collaborative analyses.
Contribute to reporting obligations, scientific deliverables, publications, and dissemination activities associated with externally funded collaborative projects.


Interaction with the EORTC network and external partners


Support disease-oriented and transversal groups in portfolio management and navigation of EORTC internal processes.
Establish and maintain high-quality collaborations with EORTC investigators, working group members, and external partners (academic, cooperative groups, industry, and EU consortia).
Represent EORTC scientific activities at group meetings, scientific events, and consortium meetings, as required.


Departmental and process-related activities


Contribute to the development, review, and continuous improvement of processes related to medical and scientific management of studies.
Participate in departmental discussions on scientific standards, methodologies, and best practices in clinical research.
Contribute to the scientific training of EORTC HQ


Profile


PhD in biomedical sciences with a minimum of 5 years of professional experience in clinical research or demonstrated experience in the development and conduct of clinical or methodological research projects; experience with EU‑funded or collaborative research initiatives is an asset.
Strong ability to draft, review, and critically assess scientific and clinical research documentation (protocols, grant applications, reports).
Process‑oriented, with the ability to work across complex organisational and governance structures.
Must be fluent in English (oral and written), other languages are an asset
Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to interact effectively with a wide range of stakeholders (academia, cooperative groups, industry, and consortia).
Ability to work independently, take initiative, and function effectively within multidisciplinary teams.
Good level of computer literacy; familiarity with clinical research systems and structured documentation is an asset.

Title: Recruitment & Admin / Office Manager
Description:
digica is a growing digital transformation consultancy firm in which every person matters.

We're not looking for someone to fill a position. We're looking for someone who loves finding the right people, building lasting relationships with consultants, and who also takes pride in keeping the house in order.




What you'll do

Recruitment & freelance talent pool

Actively source profiles on LinkedIn and job boards
Conduct qualification interviews with freelance consultants
Build and maintain a structured, living talent pool
Match available profiles with ongoing client missions
Nurture long-term relationships with consultants in the network

Admin & finance

Manage suppliers and vendors, including related invoicing
Prepare accounting documents and elements

Team life & office

Make the office a place people actually want to come to
Be the go-to person for everything that doesn't have an owner yet
Organise the moments that build team cohesion

Sales support

Bridge the gap between client needs and available profiles
Help prepare proposals and keep the CRM pipeline up to date




What we're looking for

A genuine passion for recruitment and people, that's the heart of this role
Comfortable sourcing, qualifying, following up and building a network over time
Able to handle admin in parallel without it feeling like a burden
Experience in recruitment, headhunting or talent acquisition is a real plus
Knowledge of the consulting, digital transformation or freelancing world? Strongly appreciated
Fluent in French and English. Dutch is a strong asset in our Brussels context




What you'll find here

A role blending different responsibilities with big autonomy
Direct proximity to the founder and the decisions that matter
Concrete, fast impact
A team that gives it their all and appreciates people who do the same
Based in Brussels, with 2 to 3 days per week in the office

Title: React Native Developer
Description:
React Native Developer - Brussels







About the job

Tapptic is a digital agency that sets efficient brands and products’ strategies to create outstanding ecosystems.







We are looking for a React Native Developer to join our growing team and contribute to projects across the company.




This is a full-time hybrid role located in Brussels.




The role involves developing and maintaining high-quality mobile applications using React Native, in collaboration with designers, developers, project managers, and clients.




The React Native Developer will be responsible for building performant, scalable, and maintainable cross-platform applications, integrating APIs, and contributing to technical decisions and continuous improvement of our mobile solutions.




The role will involve working collaboratively with other team members to deliver innovative digital products aligned with project requirements, timelines, and quality standards.




Qualifications:







Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
At least 2 years of experience in mobile application development with React Native
Good knowledge of TypeScript
Solid understanding of modern JavaScript/TypeScript and component-based UI development
Experience with state management libraries such as Redux or similar solutions
Experience integrating APIs such as REST and GraphQL
Awareness of cross-platform mobile development limitations, especially regarding UI
Hands-on experience using AI-assisted coding tools to support development, debugging, and code quality
Good communication and teamwork skills
French and English professional proficiency




Assets:

Experience working in Agile environments
Ability to define technical architectures adapted to project needs
Experience debugging and improving React Native application performance
Knowledge of App Store and Play Store publication processes
Experience with continuous integration
Curiosity for new technologies and willingness to continuously improve
Passion for web and mobile technologies, with strong attention to clean, reusable code




What we offer:

A collaborative and innovative work environment where each team member has the opportunity to contribute to multiple stimulating projects.
A dynamic position offering daily challenges and possibilities of evolution both in terms of competences and in terms of responsibilities.
An attractive salary package with various advantages.
A balance between homeworking and presence in the office
A friendly work environment.

Title: HR Partner (talentmanagement)
Description:
Ben jij klaar om het potentieel van onze medewerker te benutten, ontwikkelen en behouden – en zo de prestaties van onze organisatie te verbeteren? Als HR-partner talentmanagement analyseer, creëer en geef je vorm aan de loopbaan van medewerkers. Je zorgt dat we kunnen rekenen op de juiste mens op de juiste plaats.

Je werkt toekomstgericht en zorgt dat onze medewerkers klaar zijn voor de volgende stap in hun loopbaan. Dat doe je in samenwerking met diverse stakeholders.

Je bent verantwoordelijk voor een geïntegreerde aanpak van talentmanagement. Je zorgt mee voor groei en mobiliteit binnen alle fases van de loopbaan van een medewerker: van aanwerving tot exit. Je bouwt ook aan de organisatiecultuur die daarvoor nodig is. Dat betekent dus dat je zowel aandacht hebt voor het grotere plaatje als voor individuele ontwikkeling.

Wil jij impact maken door samen met ons te bouwen aan een organisatie waar medewerkers centraal staan? Solliciteer dan nu en geef mee vorm aan de toekomst van het Agentschap Integratie en Inburgering.


Je verantwoordelijkheden

Je implementeert in 2026 de medewerkersgesprekken doorheen alle fases die een medewerker doorloopt: van aanwerving in tot en met exit uit de organisatie.
Je optimaliseert de aanpak van prestatiemanagement (inclusief ontwikkeling) in de organisatie zodat dit proces tot meer toegevoegde waarde leidt in de organisatie.
Je beheert het functiehuis van de organisatie.
Je ontwikkelt een intern mobiliteitsbeleid met loopbaanpaden en retentiestrategieën.
Je bouwt op termijn inclusieve programma’s rond talentontwikkeling die diverse paden naar leiderschap en doorgroei ondersteunen.
Je bent verantwoordelijk voor het inkopen en implementeren van een geïntegreerde tool voor talentmanagement. Dat proces trek je van business case tot gefaseerde implementatie.
Je werkt intensief samen met verschillende stakeholders om bovenstaand project te laten slagen: HR-business partners, partners leren en ontwikkelen, leidinggevenden, het management, de werknemersafvaardiging …
Je hebt aandacht voor data. Geleidelijk aan integreer je waar mogelijk data-analyse in functie van een betere besluitvorming en de integratie van de bouwstenen van talentmanagement die we hierboven opsomden.


Je profiel

Je hebt minstens een bachelorsdiploma (HR, economie, psychologie, sociologie…), of gelijkwaardig door ervaring.
Je hebt minstens drie jaar ervaring in soft HR en specifiek met het opzetten van prestatiemanagement en opvolgingsplanning, inclusief tooling.
Je kan trends in verloop, betrokkenheid, en andere factoren analyseren en dashboards gebruiken om beslissingen over het personeelsbestand te onderbouwen.
Je hebt ervaring met het managen van een meerjarenproject, met een belangrijk budgettaire impact voor de organisatie.
Je kan strategisch denken. Je hebt inzicht in de organisatie en haar doelstellingen en kan de geïntegreerd talentmanagementstrategieën hierop afstemmen.
Je bent een sterke communicator. Je kan invloed uitoefenen door je boodschap af te stemmen op leidinggevenden, HR-partners en andere medewerkers. Je gebruikt bewijs om je standpunten te onderbouwen en navigeert zorgvuldig door gevoelige onderwerpen, zoals opvolging of prestaties.
Je kan goed omgaan met weerstand en je ondersteunt de managers ook in ermee om te gaan. Je kan veranderingen duidelijk communiceren.
Je werkt samen met verschillende HR-partners, leidinggevenden en het management. Je zorgt voor betrokkenheid van stakeholders. Daarbij gebruik je de gepaste methodieken om programma’s in een vroeg stadium vorm te geven.
Je bent van nature positief ingesteld en draagt met plezier bij tot de resultaten van het team.
Je hebt een uitstekende beheersing van de Nederlandse taal, zowel mondeling als schriftelijk.
Je bent digitaal vaardig en kan vlot overweg met MS Office-toepassingen: Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams … Ervaring met digitale HR-platformen is een plus.


Wat bieden we jou?

Een boeiende job in een team van enthousiaste collega’s
Voltijds contract van onbepaalde duur
Een loon volgens barema A* van het PC 329.01 (verloning op basis van relevante anciënniteit met brutobedragen tussen € 3.643,70 en € 5.670,28 op 100% tewerkstellingspercentage)
Maaltijdcheques van 6 euro per gewerkte dag
Een interessante vakantieregeling: minstens 34 verlofdagen (voltijdse tewerkstelling) en collectieve sluiting tussen Kerstmis en Nieuwjaar
Aansluiting bij vzw Sociale Dienst (persoonlijke ondersteuning en allerlei voordelen)
Glijdende werkuren en mogelijkheid tot telewerk
Hospitalisatieverzekering
Groepsverzekering
Gratis woon-werkverkeer bij gebruik van openbaar vervoer

Je standplaats is Brussel (Kanselarijstraat 17A).


Hoe solliciteer je?

Je kan enkel online solliciteren. Stel je ten laatste op 31/5/2026 kandidaat via het sollicitatieformulier (klik op de solliciteerknop).

  • Je hebt aandacht voor data. Geleidelijk aan integreer je waar mogelijk data-analyse in functie van een betere besluitvorming en de integratie van de bouwstenen van talentmanagement die we hierboven opsomden.

Je profiel

  • Je hebt minstens een bachelorsdiploma (HR, economie, psychologie, sociologie…), of gelijkwaardig door ervaring.
  • Je hebt minstens drie jaar ervaring in soft HR en specifiek met het opzetten van prestatiemanagement en opvolgingsplanning, inclusief tooling.
  • Je kan trends in verloop, betrokkenheid, en andere factoren analyseren en dashboards gebruiken om beslissingen over het personeelsbestand te onderbouwen.
  • Je hebt ervaring met het managen van een meerjarenproject, met een belangrijk budgettaire impact voor de organisatie.
  • Je kan strategisch denken. Je hebt inzicht in de organisatie en haar doelstellingen en kan de geïntegreerd talentmanagementstrategieën hierop afstemmen.
  • Je bent een sterke communicator. Je kan invloed uitoefenen door je boodschap af te stemmen op leidinggevenden, HR-partners en andere medewerkers. Je gebruikt bewijs om je standpunten te onderbouwen en navigeert zorgvuldig door gevoelige onderwerpen, zoals opvolging of prestaties.
  • Je kan goed omgaan met weerstand en je ondersteunt de managers ook in ermee om te gaan. Je kan veranderingen duidelijk communiceren.
  • Je werkt samen met verschillende HR-partners, leidinggevenden en het management. Je zorgt voor betrokkenheid van stakeholders. Daarbij gebruik je de gepaste methodieken om programma’s in een vroeg stadium vorm te geven.
  • Je bent van nature positief ingesteld en draagt met plezier bij tot de resultaten van het team.
  • Je hebt een uitstekende beheersing van de Nederlandse taal, zowel mondeling als schriftelijk.
  • Je bent digitaal vaardig en kan vlot overweg met MS Office-toepassingen: Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams … Ervaring met digitale HR-platformen is een plus.

Wat bieden we jou?

  • Een boeiende job in een team van enthousiaste collega’s
  • Voltijds contract van onbepaalde duur
  • Een loon volgens barema A* van het PC 329.01 (verloning op basis van relevante anciënniteit met brutobedragen tussen € 3.643,70 en € 5.670,28 op 100% tewerkstellingspercentage)
  • Maaltijdcheques van 6 euro per gewerkte dag
  • Een interessante vakantieregeling: minstens 34 verlofdagen (voltijdse tewerkstelling) en collectieve sluiting tussen Kerstmis en Nieuwjaar
  • Aansluiting bij vzw Sociale Dienst (persoonlijke ondersteuning en allerlei voordelen)
  • Glijdende werkuren en mogelijkheid tot telewerk
  • Hospitalisatieverzekering
  • Groepsverzekering
  • Gratis woon-werkverkeer bij gebruik van openbaar vervoer

Je standplaats is Brussel (Kanselarijstraat 17A).

Hoe solliciteer je?

Je kan enkel online solliciteren. Stel je ten laatste op 31/5/2026 kandidaat via het sollicitatieformulier (klik op de solliciteerknop).

Title: Agile Delivery Lead (freelance)
Description:
Home
Jobs


Terug naar overzicht

Agile Delivery Lead (freelance)

Help jij graag teams groeien in een veranderende context? Wil je een sleutelrol spelen in hoe datateams binnen VRT samenwerken en waarde leveren?

De afdeling Data & AI maakt een verdere evolutie in zijn werkingsmodel, en is op zoek naar een ervaren Agile Delivery Lead die teams en sleutelrollen ondersteunt in hun nieuwe manier van werken en delivery.

De context

Binnen de afdeling Data & AI- van de VRT maken we een evolutie in ons werkingsmodel , met een aangepaste team-samenstelling gelinkt aan onze doelstellingen. Die nieuw gevormde teams gaan binnenkort van start en geven hun scope, verantwoordelijkheden en manier van werken verder vorm.

Om onze werking te laten slagen, zoeken we iemand die:


Structuur brengt die teams houvast geeft én ruimte laat voor flexibiliteit
Teams begeleidt in het vinden van hun eigen effectieve werkwijze
Mensen ondersteunt in hun rol binnen een veranderende context


Wat verwachten we van jou?

Begeleiden van teams in verandering

Je helpt teams hun nieuwe werking vorm te geven en ondersteunt hen in het ontwikkelen van een duurzame manier van samenwerken, waarbij agile principes richting geven aan de aanpak.

Ondersteunen van domeinverantwoordelijken

Je Helpt Hen Concreet Bij


het opzetten en beheren van backlogs
het aanbrengen van structuur en prioriteiten
het organiseren van hun teams en werkstromen


Co-creëren van werkmethodes

Je vertrekt niet vanuit één vast framework, maar werkt samen met teams aan een aanpak die past bij hun context en noden met respect voor de agile principes.

Structuur brengen in delivery

Je helpt teams om werk gestructureerd op te nemen, te verdelen en op te volgen — met focus op transparantie en voorspelbaarheid.

Hands-on verbeteren en faciliteren

Je steekt zelf de handen uit de mouwen: je zet zaken in gang, faciliteert overlegmomenten en helpt teams vooruit door actief mee te werken aan oplossingen.

Wie zoeken we?


Je hebt ervaring als Delivery Coach, Agile Coach, Scrum Master of gelijkaardige rol, bij voorkeur binnen data-, AI- of techteams
Je hebt ervaring met organisatieverandering of teamtransformaties
Je ondersteunt graag mensen in hun rol en werkt pragmatisch mee aan oplossingen
Je brengt structuur zonder rigiditeit
Je bent sterk in faciliteren en stakeholdermanagement
Ervaring met data-omgevingen (data engineering / data science) is een plus


Waarom VRT?

Bij VRT werk je in een innovatieve omgeving waar data en AI een steeds grotere rol speelt. Je draagt bij aan een publieke organisatie met maatschappelijke impact, samen met gepassioneerde teams die streven naar kwaliteit en samenwerking.

Je Komt Terecht In Een Dynamisch Data & AI Team Met Ruimte Voor Initiatief En Verantwoordelijkheid, Gedreven Door De Maatschappelijke Opdracht Van De VRT. We Werken Vanuit Duidelijke Waarden


Betrouwbaar: Transparantie en betrouwbare data en AI staan voorop.
Begrijpelijk: We houden het helder en toegankelijk.
Empowerend: We creëren waarde voor alle activiteiten van onze organisatie


Wat bieden wij jou?

Je krijgt een voltijds contract voor een freelance opdracht via tussenpersoon en dit voor 12 maanden. In onderling overleg is verlenging mogelijk (Max. 3 maal verlengbaar).

De opdracht wordt gegund aan 1 of meer partijen.

Interesse?

Dit is een zelfstandige contract via tussenpersoon. De VRT maakt voor dit selectieproces gebruik van de diensten van MSP-partner Solvus. Voor deze opdracht krijg je bijgevolg een contract aangeboden met Solvus, niet rechtsreeks met VRT.

Als je op ‘Solliciteren’ klikt word je doorgestuurd naar de marktplaats van ‘Connecting-Expertise’. Maak een leveranciersaccount aan of log in op de marktplaats als je al een account hebt. Klik linksboven in het menu op de tab “requests & proposals”. Klik vervolgens op de tab “received requests” waar je een overzicht van je aanvragen kan terugvinden. Zoek in het overzicht op VRT[referentienummer] (bv: VRT123456). Via de tab “create a proposal” in de aanvraag is het mogelijk om een offerte met je CV en motivatiebrief in te indienen.

VRT zet zich actief in om een inclusieve omgeving te voorzien voor alle medewerkers. We zijn er als publieke omroep voor iedereen in Vlaanderen, daarom vinden we het belangrijk om in ons personeel ook de diversiteit te zien die onze samenleving rijk is. Iedereen verschillend, iedereen welkom!

Solliciteren

Title: Room Attendant / Night Cleaner (M/F/X)
Description:
About Thon Hotels


Engaged for the long term. We offer more than a job; we offer a place where you can feel at home, grow, and build a career.
We brighten your day. Because when you feel supported at work, it shows, in every guest experience.
Innovative. We constantly improve and invest in our hotels.
Financially solid. An international company you can rely on.
Green. We matter to contribute to a more sustainable society for future generations being locally and internationally certified with green labels.


We are currently looking for a Room Attendant / Night Cleaner to join our dynamic team at Thon Hotel Bristol Stephanie.


Great location. Situated on the famous Avenue Louise.
Welcoming company culture: One of the smaller Thon Hotels in Brussels
Thon Residence Florence : We also take care of the guests of this Residence


Why join us ?


Family mindset. At Thon Hotels you are not a number, but an individual with your own skills, talents, and passion.
Diversity. Our guests and your future colleagues come from all over the world.
Flat hierarchy. No useless bureaucracy & corporate mentality.
Autonomy. You are the actor of your career by doing the extra step without fear.
Accessible. Our hotels are easily accessible in Brussels.


Do ?


Clean. You ensure that our rooms, apartments and public areas are always neat for our guests to arrive so they feel right at home.
Observe. You never miss a spot! Even while walking through the hallways and the public spaces you recognise every speck of dust.
Product knowledge. You know exactly which cleaning supply to use for any surface.
Health & Safety. You know the rules and regulations and always keep them in mind while doing your job.
Simply do it. You love giving a great service, each and every day.
Have fun. Having fun while working hard is what you can expect to find with our amazing, vibrant and international team.


Our perfect match ?


Clean. Hygiene is the rule: You live it and keep yourself up to date.
Motivated. Experience is a plus; showing us you are motivated is even more important.
Customer oriented. Making people happy is your priority.
Flexible. No 9-5 mentality. You don’t mind working flexible schedules, including nights, bank holidays and weekends.
Team player. You work well individually as well as part of a team.
Accurate. You have an eye for detail.


Receive ?


Replacement contract for undetermined duration.
Motivational remuneration package including:
Contribution to public transportation costs. And yes, we contribute for 100%.
Flexible extra-legal benefits. Pick and choose between an attendance bonus, extra days off or a cafeteria plan.
Hospitalization insurance. And yes, also for the people you love who live at the same address.
Seniority days. Extra days off according to your seniority and age.
Meal voucher. We provide a meal voucher per worked day.
Personal development. You can choose trainings through our catalogue.
Celebrate. We encourage to celebrate successes with your team at staff events.
Family event. You will be invited a family event where you and relatives are honoured.
Fun. Excellent working atmosphere with the nicest colleagues.

Title: Pharmacien Adjoint
Description:
Mission

Gérer les activités de la pharmacie sous la supervision du titulaire, incluant stocks, commandes, caisse et crédits.

Responsabilités

Encadrer et superviser le personnel selon les normes de bien-être au travail et de productivité tout en maintenant une bonne ambiance de travail.
Faire respecter les valeurs internes à la société, ainsi que la législation, la réglementation et la déontologie propres à la profession en collaboration avec le titulaire.
Contrôler les ordonnances, réceptionner et enregistrer les prescriptions médicales, et délivrer les médicaments. Superviser ou effectuer les préparations magistrales ou officinales.
Garantir un service à la patientèle professionnel, courtois et discret, en offrant des conseils pharmaceutiques.
Participer à l'écolage de nouveaux collaborateurs.




Profil

Tu es titulaire d'un diplôme en sciences pharmaceutiques.
Tu es flexible au niveau des horaires et tu acceptes d’effectuer des gardes dans le cas d'un empêchement du titulaire.
Tu es une personne très consciencieuse et tu n'hésites pas à faire remonter toutes les informations importantes au titulaire, liées au bon fonctionnement interne de la pharmacie.
Tu aimes travailler en équipe, tu es serviable et à l'écoute des autres, tu es rigoureux, polyvalent, discret, organisé, autonome et tu gères facilement ton stress
Tu apprécies les contacts humains et tu possèdes une communication aisée.




Offre

Contrat à durée indéterminée (30h/semaine)
Salaire attractif complété d'avantages extra-légaux (assurance groupe, chèques-repas, assurance hospitalisation à un tarif avantageux, ...)
Réduction de 30% dans nos pharmacies Familia
Formations nécessaires à la bonne exécution de la fonction (SSFP, formation interne, gestion d'équipe, ...)




Intéressé(e)?

Envoie ton CV et lettre de motivation par mail avec la mention : "Candidature - Pharmacien adjoint Uccle Prince" à l'adresse suivante : recrutement@pharmacie-familia.be

Title: Policy & Data Analyst (Data Scientist) - Hybrid
HTML:
For an European institution in Brussels, Belgium, we are looking for a hybrid Policy & Data Analyst (Data Scientist). We are looking for a Policy & Data Analyst with a strong understanding of political and policy environments, combined with the ability to work with data to support decision-making. This role is ideal for profiles such as political scientists or policy specialists who are comfortable using data and visualisation tools to generate insights


Candidates need to be based in Europe within 2 hours of Brussels.

Candidates need to be fluent in English. A work permit is required, but not provided. This position is open for contractors.


Tasks and Responsibilities:

  • Analyse policy-related data to support strategic decision-making;
  • Contribute to the development and evaluation of policies using data-driven insights;
  • Translate complex data into clear narratives tailored to policy and non-technical audiences;
  • Develop and maintain simple dashboards and reports to monitor key indicators;
  • Ensure that data analysis aligns with policy frameworks, governance standards, and institutional priorities;
  • Engage with stakeholders to communicate findings and support evidence-based policy discussions;
  • Contribute to knowledge sharing and capacity building across teams;


Profile:

  • Bachelor or Master degree;
  • +6 years of IT experience;
  • +3–5 years of professional experience in policy analysis, public sector, consulting, or similar environments;
  • Strong background in political science, public policy, economics, or a related field;
  • Experience working with policy frameworks, governance, or regulatory environments;
  • Ability to analyse and interpret data in a policy context;
  • Familiarity with data visualisation or reporting tools (e.g., Power BI or similar);
  • Good understanding of structured data and basic querying (e.g., SQL is a plus, not mandatory);
  • Exposure to data analysis or business intelligence tools
  • Experience working in international, governmental, or institutional contexts is a plus
  • Familiarity with data-driven policy evaluation or impact assessment methodologies
  • Exposure to data analysis or business intelligence tools
  • Fluent in English;

Title: Productiemanager
Description:
Feneko is een Belgische producent van bouwmaterialen in gelakt of bekleed aluminium, gericht op accessoires voor het afwerken van ramen en deuren.

Onze unieke "one-stop-shop" ervaring laat elke professionele schrijnwerker toe de nodige accessoires te bestellen voor de totaalafwerking van een residentiële gevel, van vliegenramen en -deuren, sandwichpanelen of decoratieve deurpanelen tot plooiwerk zoals raamtabletten, dakranden en muurkappen.

Om deze ervaring te ondersteunen investeert Feneko voortdurend in een verbeterde informatisering en automatisatie, gekoppeld aan een performante logistieke service om onze klanten snel te beleveren.

 

Functie:

Wij zoeken een Production Manager die verantwoordelijk is voor de kwaliteit en efficiëntie van onze verschillende productiesites binnen de groep.

Je analyseert waar processen beter kunnen, achterhaalt de oorzaken en zorgt ervoor dat verbeteringen ook effectief worden doorgevoerd op de werkvloer. Je brengt structuur en stemt de werking tussen de verschillende productiesites op elkaar af en zorgt voor meer uniformiteit. Je werkt nauw samen met de Operations Managers (productie), binnen een omgeving van productie op maat van de klant (high mix/ low volume).

Jouw verantwoordelijkheden

·        Productieproblemen analyseren en oplossen op de werkvloer

·        Doorvoeren van structurele verbeteringen

·        Processen efficiënter en consistenter maken over de sites

·        Capaciteit en planning afstemmen tussen de productiesites

·        Operations Managers ondersteunen en bijsturen

·        Nieuwe machines en werkwijzen implementeren

 

Profiel:

Je hebt een master- of bacheloropleiding in een technische richting.

Je hebt ruime ervaring in een productieomgeving (bij voorkeur multi-site).

Je combineert strategisch inzicht met een hands-on mentaliteit.

Je hebt sterke analytische vaardigheden en denkt in processen en optimalisatie.

Je hebt kennis van productieomgevingen met variatie (high mix/ low volume is een plus).

Je bent een natuurlijke leider die mensen kan verbinden en meenemen in verandering.

Je communiceert vlot in Nederlands en Frans.

HTML:
Feneko is een Belgische producent van bouwmaterialen in gelakt of bekleed aluminium, gericht op accessoires voor het afwerken van ramen en deuren.

Onze unieke "one-stop-shop" ervaring laat elke professionele schrijnwerker toe de nodige accessoires te bestellen voor de totaalafwerking van een residentiële gevel, van vliegenramen en -deuren, sandwichpanelen of decoratieve deurpanelen tot plooiwerk zoals raamtabletten, dakranden en muurkappen.

Om deze ervaring te ondersteunen investeert Feneko voortdurend in een verbeterde informatisering en automatisatie, gekoppeld aan een performante logistieke service om onze klanten snel te beleveren.

 

Functie:

Wij zoeken een Production Manager die verantwoordelijk is voor de kwaliteit en efficiëntie van onze verschillende productiesites binnen de groep.

Je analyseert waar processen beter kunnen, achterhaalt de oorzaken en zorgt ervoor dat verbeteringen ook effectief worden doorgevoerd op de werkvloer. Je brengt structuur en stemt de werking tussen de verschillende productiesites op elkaar af en zorgt voor meer uniformiteit. Je werkt nauw samen met de Operations Managers (productie), binnen een omgeving van productie op maat van de klant (high mix/ low volume).

Jouw verantwoordelijkheden

·        Productieproblemen analyseren en oplossen op de werkvloer

·        Doorvoeren van structurele verbeteringen

·        Processen efficiënter en consistenter maken over de sites

·        Capaciteit en planning afstemmen tussen de productiesites

·        Operations Managers ondersteunen en bijsturen

·        Nieuwe machines en werkwijzen implementeren

 

Profiel:

Je hebt een master- of bacheloropleiding in een technische richting.

Je hebt ruime ervaring in een productieomgeving (bij voorkeur multi-site).

Je combineert strategisch inzicht met een hands-on mentaliteit.

Je hebt sterke analytische vaardigheden en denkt in processen en optimalisatie.

Je hebt kennis van productieomgevingen met variatie (high mix/ low volume is een plus).

Je bent een natuurlijke leider die mensen kan verbinden en meenemen in verandering.

Je communiceert vlot in Nederlands en Frans.

Title: AI Architect
Description:
Company Description

Sopra Steria offers tailored, end-to-end corporate technology and software solutions to help clients make bold choices and deliver results. Successfully so! With more than 56.000 colleagues in 30 countries, we rank as Europe’s leading digital solutions provider. Some of the most successful companies in Europe rely on our technology due to our commitment to innovation, collaboration, and value in business development.

The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together.

Job Description

As an AI Architect, you'll delve into the core of AI, applying your skills across domains like healthcare, transportation, and chatbot development. You'll routinely work with techniques like computer vision, NLP, forecasting, optimization, and generative AI, tackling real-world scenarios without getting lost in complexity. You'll translate business challenges into AI solutions, aligning them with market needs and inspiring both employees and companies. You'll lead project teams to implement these solutions, while considering legal, ethical, and moral boundaries.

You collaborate with data scientists and AI experts to identify and pilot AI use cases, ensuring they reach production readiness. Align with business teams to convert leaders' visions into practical, feasible technical solutions. You gather input from stakeholders and develop processes and products that meet current and future needs. You define the AI architecture and choose technologies from open-source or commercial options. Select deployment models (cloud, on-premises, or hybrid) for seamless integration with existing data and analytics tools.

You improve AI tools and practices, focusing on continuous improvement. Implement feedback mechanisms to assess and recalibrate models as needed. You serve as an AI expert, generating and sharing innovative ideas and insights with colleagues and customers through training and knowledge-sharing sessions.

Qualifications

We’re seeking passionate colleagues who are eager to push the boundaries in digital transformation and technology consulting. At Sopra Steria, you’ll have the opportunity to grow your skills in a constructive, collaborative team environment, working on impactful projects that drive change for our clients. If you thrive on challenge and meet (most of) the qualifications below, we look forward to your application!

You’ll have knowledge and experience of the following: 


At least 7+ years of experience in AI and Data Science.
Multiple examples where you coordinated a team in building end-to-end AI solutions.
Experienced in developing (deep learning) models.
Experience in securely and rapidly developing, training, evaluating, debugging, and deploying AI models in both practical and theoretical scenarios.
Experience with modern computer vision tools for image classification, object detection, tracking, and segmentation.
Expertise in working with LLMs (GPT, BERT, etc.) and are familiar with NLP and LLM evaluation metrics.
Hands-on experience in the development of AI solutions in cloud environments like Microsoft AWS or azure.
You approach problem-solving with an open-minded and constructive attitude.
You have the drive to stay up to date with the fast-changing AI landscape.
You're an excellent mentor who not only designs the architecture but also ensures our consultants implement it successfully.
Conduct thorough code reviews, particularly in Python, to ensure code quality and adherence to best practices.
Guide consultants from Proof of Concept (POC) to production, leveraging your infrastructure knowledge to support deployment and optimization.
You are fluent in Dutch and English. Knowledge of French is a plus.
You are resident in Belgium and look forward to working a limited days a week at the client's or our offices.
You have experience in improving AI/ML strategies.


What We Can Offer You

As a member of one of Europe’s largest digital solutions providers, you’ll benefit from extensive career development opportunities, both local and international. At the Sopra Steria Academy, you’ll be part of a dynamic network of 56,000 professionals at all stages of their careers. With a wide array of offices to explore, you can find your ideal location and take the next step in your career. 

You’ll become a part of a major Tech player in Europe recognized for its consulting, digital services, and software development. On top of that, you’ll join the OptimAi Team: the partner for advanced, intelligent solutions using AI.

Additional Information

Our employees are known for making bold choices and delivering results. Work among high-level professionals at the forefront of corporate software solutions and innovation at Europe’s Leading Digital Service Provider.  

We offer a generous employee benefits package that includes:


A company car or mobility budget.
Laptop, phone & phone subscription.
A competitive salary and indefinite contract.
Insurance coverage, meal vouchers, eco-cheques, and more.
Continuous learning opportunities through the Sopra Steria Academy to support your career development.


People are the cornerstone of our success. That’s why we aspire to be bolder together. Our goal is to build high-functioning teams and healthy team environments that inspire and help each other to deliver excellence for each of our customers.  

Excited about this job opportunity? Ready to shape the world with us? Great! We are looking forward to your application! 

______

Sopra Steria is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, ancestry, nationality, color, family or medical leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. We foster a work environment that is inclusive and respectful of all differences.
